name: bagit-profile

metadata.name: bagit-profile
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: This module can be used to validate BagitProfiles.

documentation:
# Bagit Profile (validator)

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/bagit_profile.svg
:target: https://pypi.org/project/bagit_profile/
```

```{image} https://circleci.com/gh/bagit-profiles/bagit-profiles-validator.svg?style=svg
:target: https://circleci.com/gh/bagit-profiles/bagit-profiles-validator
```

## Description

A simple Python module for validating BagIt profiles. See the [BagIt
Profiles Specification](https://github.com/bagit-profiles/bagit-profiles/blob/master/README.md)
for more information.

This module is intended for use with
[bagit-python](https://github.com/LibraryOfCongress/bagit-python) but does not extend it.

## Installation

`bagit_profile.py` is a single-file python module that you can drop into
your project as needed or you can install globally with:

1. `git clone https://github.com/bagit-profiles/bagit-profiles-validator.git`
2. `cd bagit-profiles-validator`
3. `sudo python setup.py install`

or:

`pip install bagit_profile`

## Usage

```python
import bagit
import bagit_profile
```

Instantiate an existing Bag using
[bagit](https://github.com/LibraryOfCongress/bagit-python).
`python bag = bagit.Bag('mydir')`

Instantiate a profile, supplying its URI.
`python my_profile = bagit_profile.Profile('http://example.com/bagitprofile.json')`

Validate 'Serialization' and 'Accept-Serialization'. This must be done
before .validate(bag) is called. 'mydir' is the path to the Bag.

```python
if my_profile.validate_serialization('mydir'):
    print "Serialization validates"
else:
    print "Serialization does not validate"
```

Validate the rest of the profile.

```python
if my_profile.validate(bag):
    print "Validates"
else:
    print "Does not validate"
```

Or from the commandline:

`bagit_profile.py 'http://uri.for.profile/profile.json' path/to/bag`

## Test suite

`python setup.py test`

## Development

1. [Fork the
   repository](https://help.github.com/articles/fork-a-repo)
2. Do something awesome!
3. [Submit a pull
   request](https://help.github.com/articles/creating-a-pull-request)
   explianing what your code does

modules.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile
documentation:
A simple Python module for validating BagIt profiles. See
https://github.com/bagit-profiles/bagit-profiles
for more information.

This module is intended for use with https://github.com/edsu/bagit but does not extend it.

Usage:

import bagit
import bagit_profile

# Instantiate an existing Bag using https://github.com/edsu/bagit.
bag = bagit.Bag('mydir')

# Instantiate a profile, supplying its URI.
my_profile = bagit_profile.Profile('http://example.com/bagitprofile.json')

# Validate 'Serialization' and 'Accept-Serialization'. This must be done
# before .validate(bag) is called. 'mydir' is the path to the Bag.
if my_profile.validate_serialization('mydir'):
    print "Serialization validates"
else:
    print "Serialization does not validate"

# Validate the rest of the profile.
if my_profile.validate(bag):
    print "Validates"
else:
    print "Does not validate"
!python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.fnmatch_any
documentation: Return true if any of the pattern fnmatches a file path

functions.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.find_tag_files
documentation: Find tag files

classes.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.ProfileValidationError

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.ProfileValidationError.__init__
documentation: TODO: or just 'pass' instead of __init__ and __str__
Define an exceptin class for use within this module.

classes.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate
documentation: Call all the validate functions other than validate_bagit_profile(),
which we've already called. 'Serialization' and 'Accept-Serialization'
are validated in validate_serialization().

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_bagit_profile
documentation: Set default values for unspecified tags and validate the profile itself.

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_bagit_profile_info
documentation: Check self.profile['bag-profile-info'] to see if "Source-Organization",
"External-Description", "Version" and "BagIt-Profile-Identifier" are present.

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_bagit_profile_accept_bagit_versions
documentation: Ensure all versions in 'Accept-BagIt-Version' are strings

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_bag_info
documentation: Validate tags in self.profile['Bag-Info'].

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.normalize_tag
documentation: Normalize to canonical lowercase, if profile is ignoring bag-info.txt tag case.

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_manifests_required
documentation: For each member of self.profile['manifests_required'], throw an exception if
the manifest file is not present.

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_tag_manifests_required
documentation: For each member of self.profile['tag_manifests_required'], throw an exception if
the tag manifest file is not present.

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_tag_files_allowed
documentation: Validate the `Tag-Files-Allowed` tag.

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_tag_files_required
documentation: For each member of self.profile['Tag-Files-Required'], throw an exception if
the path does not exist.

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_allow_fetch
documentation: Check to see if this constraint is False, and if it is, then check to see
if the fetch.txt file exists. If it does, throw an exception.

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_accept_bagit_version
documentation: Check the Bag's version, and if it's not in the list of allowed versions,
throw an exception.

methods.name: bagit_profile-1.3.1.data.scripts.bagit_profile.Profile.validate_serialization
documentation: Perform tests on 'Serialization' and 'Accept-Serialization', in one function.
Since https://github.com/edsu/bagit can't tell us if a Bag is serialized or
not, we need to pass this function the path to the Bag, not the object. Also,
this method needs to be called before .validate().
Define the Profile class.

modules.name: bagit_profile
documentation:
A simple Python module for validating BagIt profiles. See
https://github.com/bagit-profiles/bagit-profiles
for more information.

This module is intended for use with https://github.com/edsu/bagit but does not extend it.

Usage:

import bagit
import bagit_profile

# Instantiate an existing Bag using https://github.com/edsu/bagit.
bag = bagit.Bag('mydir')

# Instantiate a profile, supplying its URI.
my_profile = bagit_profile.Profile('http://example.com/bagitprofile.json')

# Validate 'Serialization' and 'Accept-Serialization'. This must be done
# before .validate(bag) is called. 'mydir' is the path to the Bag.
if my_profile.validate_serialization('mydir'):
    print "Serialization validates"
else:
    print "Serialization does not validate"

# Validate the rest of the profile.
if my_profile.validate(bag):
    print "Validates"
else:
    print "Does not validate"
!/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
